para_trans: To the lands owned by the Railway Department
Village Map and Records
(Village Sample 7/92, Income Card) with separate Survey Number / Group Number / Town Geometric Number to register ownership as "Government of India Railway Department."
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Government Decision Number: Sink-2025 / Q. 236 / Geometrics / E-1346038 1st Floor, Main Building, Mantralaya,
Madame Cama Marg, Martyrdom Rajguru Chowk, Mumbai-400032
Date: 7th May 2026
The Proposal -

In the state of Maharashtra, private lands have been acquired by the railway department for various purposes, including railways. Also, many railway lines have been in existence since the pre-independence period. The first survey in the state of Maharashtra was conducted during the period 1890-1930 CE, in accordance with the revenue laws and regulations in force at that time. The area, size, and boundaries of each village have been demarcated according to the number seven at the time of the original survey and demarcation. Village names with number seven and authority records have been prepared for each village. However, at the time of the original survey and consolidation, the railway records of the area have been taken over by the Railways. However, the land rights of railway owners are not recorded in the title deeds.

Section-79 of the Maharashtra Land Revenue Act 1966 provides that the state government may initiate land enumeration in any part of the state and that a land assessment made before the commencement of the Maharashtra Land Revenue Act under section-89 shall be deemed to have been done under this section. Also, the Sub-Divisional Rules of Geo-Metric Number and Geo-Metric Number, 1969 prescribe the procedure for revenue geometries.

The pre-existing railway zone of the original Survey B block in the state of Maharashtra is not recorded in the Village Map, Rights Record (Village Model 9/22, Income Card) with respect to owner rights in the name of Railways. In the preparation of records after the original land assessment, mainly, 1) Railway lines are shown in the village map, but the authority record is not created, while the area of the railway is marked in the Goshwa of the layout.
2) The village map / map of some villages has replaced the railway line with a road, Sadak.
Government Decision Number: Sink 2025 / Q. 236 / Geometrics / E-1346038

3) In villages where consolidation / regroupment plan has been implemented after the original survey, the village map prepared after consolidation plan / regroupment plan specifies the extent and area of the railway line. However, ownership does not appear to be recorded in the title deed (village pattern 7/12, income card).

4) After the original survey, private land has been acquired for the railway line. However, village maps and authority records (village pattern 7/12, income sheet) of villages have not been updated as per the edits.

For the above reason, the name of the railway department is not recorded on the authority record (village pattern 7/12, income sheet). This has led to court claims regarding encroachment by adjacent holders. Some cases are justifiable. Therefore, the Ministry of Railways, Government of India is communicating to the Office of the Commissioner of Deposits, Department of Railways regarding updating of village maps, authority records by inserting "Government of India Railways" in the Land Rights Record (Village Model 7/12, Income Card) of Railway Owners.

As mentioned above, there are disputes regarding the ownership and jurisdiction of the railway's area as it has not been operationalized. Hence, the title deed (village pattern 7/12, income card) village map must include the name of "Government of India Railways" in the title deed and it is the responsibility of the government to protect and conserve the administrative lands. Considering all these factors, the matter of taking records of the name "Government of India Railways" on the title record (village pattern 7/12, income card) was under the consideration of the government. The government is now taking the following decisions in this regard.
The Government's Decision-

1. Reviewing the original survey number maps, formatted at the time of original inspection and demarcation, the village map / settlement map of the original SWAY number shows the railway route and the SWAY number of SALs are fixed along the railway line. Also, the entire village appears to be entering another village by showing the railway line in a row in the map. In such a case, the railway line boundary in the village map should be closed in a bonnet manner, and the authority record (village pattern 7/12) of the area shown to the railway in the original configuration of the railway should be opened. While preparing the zone-wise break-up of the train (village pattern 7/92), a minimum sheet should be prepared and the last number / group number should be given in the original revenue village map in the format B and the holder should be noted as "Government of India Railway." Also, in the retention type, state "Government of India Railways," the total area of the configuration in Rakkana No. 3, and in Rakkana No. 4, the abdominal area. Also, the area in the enclosure should be reduced to include the last zero number / group number in the plot of the geometric number of the enclosure.
The District Superintendent of Land Records should take action to prepare a minimum sheet.

Based on the minimum sheet B map, the Talathi should record their village name, village pattern-I, size-I with red ink, open a separate rights record (village pattern 7/12) and write "Government of India Railways" on it.
Page 2 of 5
Government Decision Number: Sink 2025 / Q. 236 / Geometrics / E-1346038

In the state of Maharashtra, the village where the consolidation scheme and the regroupment scheme are applicable in the Nagpur division. The village map prepared after the consolidation plan and re-counting plan shows that the railway line boundary and the demarcation area, the demarcation number at the time of the original survey and demarcation, and the demarcation area, have been retained. However, there is no record of the location of the train as far as freight is concerned. In such a case, a separate title record (village pattern 7/92) of the area should be prepared and the name "Government of India Railways" filed on it depending on the proceedings as mentioned in the above paragraph no.

3. The original survey b. The extent of the map drawn at the time of aggregation, the extent of the railway route shown in the rural area drawn up after the aggregation plan / census plan, the lands acquired by the railway department for the extension of the railway line during the implementation of the aggregation plan. Accordingly, a revised railway line demarcation is shown in the group number map / re-polling village map. But after the consolidation plan / recalculation plan, the area of the sampling area is shown as the map / sampling of the survey number of the earlier original survey without modifying it. In such cases, depending on the procedure as mentioned in paragraph No. 01 above confirming the area, the separate jurisdiction record of the area (village model. 7/12) should be generated and the name "Government of India Railways" should be entered on it.

4. The original survey has been done after the acquisition of land for the railways and accordingly, a minimum sheet has been prepared. However, while preparing the minimum sheet, the area is shown to the Railways in the original area in the minimum sheet envelope, without the authority of the edited area being recorded in the record (village pattern 7/92). In such a case, the shape and village map may be recorded as "Government of India Railways," with the holder denoting the area edited in the authority record (village pattern 7/12) by executing a minimal sheet.

5. In the state of Maharashtra, after the first survey, the railway line has been expanded and new railway lines have been started. For this, land has been acquired and based on that, the railway line has been extended after land acquisition by the railway department. However, records of land acquired for railway line, new railway station, etc. have not been updated in many villages, revenue village records, authority records (village pattern 7/12 Wab income cards). In such cases, revenue village maps and authority records (village pattern 7/12 b income register) should be updated by obtaining documents such as land acquisition judgment, joint counting report, maps, possession receipt, remuneration register, etc. from the office of the Collector or the concerned land acquisition officer and accordingly preparing the land acquisition more or less sheet. For this, action should be taken on the basis of circulars issued from time to time.

6. Railway AMA road, route is mentioned in the village map while verifying the records of the land acquired by the railway department for the respective railway lines, railway stations, etc. In addition, the area of the railway is included in the area of other lines in the framework of the configuration.
Page 3 of 5
Government Decision Number: Sink 2025 / Q. 236 / Geometrics / E-1346038

It has come. In this regard, the proposal for alteration of the original survey number from the level of District Superintendent Land Records to the original number map, annotated extracts, formatted records, verification based on these, ensuring that the original survey number is changed to village names, formatted, etc., should be approved by the Collector under Section 155 of the Maharashtra Land Revenue Act, 1966 and updated as mentioned in the above paragraph 19, based on the procedure, village maps, authority records (village pattern\ 9/22).

7. The railway track after enumeration is shown in the village map during the original geo-measurement and survey. However, its area is not shown in the map. In some villages, the railway track is marked with a broken line, the area of which is mentioned in the registration form Shera Rakanya to the railways. Over time, SEPA has been discontinued and the village model 9/22 has come into existence. The area of the railway line shown in the title deed form is shown as poor in the village pattern 7/12. However, the name of the railway has not been recorded in the village sample 7/12 owner's name. Therefore, the leftover registration of the railway area must be taken into the ownership area in the village model 7/12 as on the title deed. Also, the village map should show the demarcation line along the firm line and act accordingly.

8. In the state of Maharashtra, a railway line passes through the city limits of the village where Town Geometries, Town Geometries, Village Geometries survey are carried out in the villages where Village / Geometries are applicable. There are also railway stations and other railway divisions. The area of the site is defined by delimitation in the Town Geometrical Graph. The name of the "Government of India Railways" should be registered with respect to ownership by ensuring such a place, opening an income card. However, in cases where the village in which the Town Geometries, Village Geometries Survey has been carried out, the railway line passing through the city limits, the extent of the other railway station, the area, has not been determined, the District Superintendent of Land Records, after receiving proposals from the Railway Department regarding the railway station in the village, Peth, Town, shall take action to update the Town Geometries, the income statement, after obtaining the approval of the Collector under section 155 of the Maharashtra Land Revenue Act, 1966. During this process, if a dispute arises regarding the boundary and area, the Commissioner and the Director of Land Records (Maharashtra State), Pune, shall take action in accordance with the circular.

9. In order to carry out all the aforesaid activities, the Collector shall take action to update the village maps, authority records (village pattern 7/12, income statement) of the land held by the Railway Department by conducting a joint special drive of the Revenue Department and the Land Records Department.
